question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026, showing that CPI increased only 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis. This is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for the market to resolve YES. Since the actual data is now available and shows CPI rose only 0.3%, the probability of this market resolving YES is 0%.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing 0.3% March CPI increase
Market resolution is now deterministic based on official data
risks: Extremely remote possibility of data revision
